items.title: Trump tells BBC that King's visit could 'absolutely' help repair relations with UK
items.summary: In a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor, the president discussed next week's visit and his relationship with the UK PM.

items.title: Epstein housed abuse victims in London flats, BBC reveals
items.summary: The revelations intensify concerns about repeated police decisions not to investigate 2015 trafficking claims.

items.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after  mocking Nigerians' spoken English
items.summary: William Ruto says Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world" while it is hard to understand Nigerians.

items.title: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses
items.summary: The woman got stuck waist-deep in a pit latrine in the Australian outback after it gave way.

items.title: South Korea police arrest man for posting AI photo of runaway wolf
items.summary: The widely circulated image had prompted authorities to move their search operation.

items.title: Ringo Starr: 'I made all my mistakes on stage'
items.summary: Beatles star reveals all about his new country album, being a Sir and coming up with the phrase A Hard Day's Night.

items.title: Aboriginal children's book pulled over illustrator's Bondi attack comments
items.summary: Several writers have cut ties with the University of Queensland Press for cancelling the book.

items.title: US soldier charged after winning $400,000 betting on removal of Maduro
items.summary: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket on the basis of classified information, the justice department says.

items.title: Huge chunk of glacier blocks Everest route in peak climbing season
items.summary: Sherpas cannot prepare the route to the world's highest peak because a huge block of ice is in the way.

items.title: Singer D4vd had 'significant amount' of child sex abuse images when arrested, prosecutors say
items.summary: The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

items.title: Meta to cut one in 10 jobs after spending billions on AI
items.summary: The cuts, which employees had been expecting for weeks, will be Meta's largest layoff since 2023.

items.title: White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms
items.summary: A memo from Michael Kratsios says firms, mainly in China, are wrongfully distilling US AI models.

items.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
items.summary: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

items.title: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
items.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

items.title: US government watchdog to investigate Epstein files release
items.summary: The justice department's inspector general will review the withholding and overdue release of secret files.

items.title: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
items.summary: Opposition parties accused security forces but the official report does not say who was responsible.

items.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving five critically hurt
items.summary: The accident happened early on Thursday at a level crossing north of Copenhagen.

items.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve Paramount's $111bn takeover
items.summary: The approval came as Donald Trump is to attend a dinner with billionaire Paramount backers the Ellisons.

items.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
items.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

items.title: Steve Rosenberg: Kremlin's tightening grip on internet fuels Russian discontent
items.summary: Officials say the restrictions are for public safety, but businesses and the public are feeling the impact.

items.title: Masked Iranian forces appear to seize ships in staged video
items.summary: Analysis indicates that parts of the video appear to have been filmed hours after the ships were reportedly seized.

items.title: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
items.summary: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

items.title: A rumour, a lynching in India and a long wait for justice
items.summary: A court has convicted 20 people for the 2018 mob lynching of two men falsely accused of kidnapping children.

items.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
items.summary: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

items.title: My five-minute phone call with President Trump
items.summary: Sarah Smith asked the president about King Charles's upcoming visit, the state of the so-called special relationship and the war with Iran.

items.title: Hundreds of wildfires burn across Florida and Georgia
items.summary: Officials in both states cite dry conditions, high winds and drought as key factors making the fires difficult to control.

items.title: BBC visits migrant camp in northern France as new deal announced
items.summary: A proposed three-year deal with Paris is aimed at stopping migrants crossing the Channel on small boats.

items.title: Hot air balloon makes emergency landing in California backyard
items.summary: "Look at that, wow!" - A couple in Temecula woke up to find a balloon boarded with 13 people behind their home on Saturday.

items.title: Are insider traders making millions from the Iran war?
items.summary: The BBC has found significant spikes in activity shortly before the US president made some announcements.
